name: Salt de Sallent - circular from Rupit
name_original: Salt de Sallent - circular desde Rupit
region_intro: Hiking around Rupit I Pruit offers exploration of a landscape characterized by a rocky mountain ridge, dense woodlands, and impressive crags within the Collsacabra Natural Park. The region features streams, sheer cliffs, and waterfalls, including the notable Salt de Sallent. Deciduous forests of beech and oak, alongside mountain holm oak, cover varied terrain suitable for hiking. Best hiking trails around Rupit I Pruit The most popular hiking route is Salto de Sallent and Sant Joan de Fàbregues - Circular from Rupit, a 4.0 miles (6.5 km) trail that takes about 2 hours to complete. This easy route combines views of the Salt de Sallent waterfall with the historic Sant Joan de Fàbregues church. Another top favourite among local hikers is Salt de Sallent - circular from Rupit, a moderate 3.9 miles (6.3 km) path. This trail leads directly to the impressive Salt de Sallent waterfall, offering views of its over 100-meter drop. Local hikers also love the Sallent Waterfall – Rupit Village loop from Rupit, a 2.8 miles (4.5 km) trail leading through forests and past the waterfall, often completed in about 1 hour 25 minutes. Hiking around Rupit I Pruit is defined by dramatic waterfalls, ancient woodlands, and rocky mountain ridges. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy village loops to moderate trails exploring natural features. The routes in Rupit I Pruit are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 440 reviews. More than 2,700 hikers have used komoot to explore Rupit I Pruit's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Rupit I Pruit?
A: There are over 30 hiking trails in the Rupit I Pruit area, offering a diverse range of experiences. These routes traverse the stunning landscapes of the Collsacabra Natural Park, featuring rocky ridges, dense woodlands, and impressive waterfalls.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of hiking trails in Rupit I Pruit?
A: The trails around Rupit I Pruit cater to various fitness levels. You'll find 14 easy routes, 15 moderate routes, and 3 more challenging options, ensuring there's a suitable path for almost everyone.

Q: Are there any trails that feature the Salt de Sallent waterfall?
A: Yes, the impressive Salt de Sallent waterfall, Catalonia's highest with a drop of over 100 meters, is a highlight of many trails. You can explore it on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e980977251/salt-de-sallent-circular-from-rupit" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt de Sallent - circular from Rupit&lt;/a&gt;, which offers spectacular views, especially after rain. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1476688" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sallent Waterfall – Rupit Village loop from Rupit&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Rupit I Pruit?
A: Many of the trails in Rupit I Pruit are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e938472893/salto-de-sallent-and-sant-joan-de-fabregues-circular-from-rupit" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto de Sallent and Sant Joan de Fàbregues - Circular from Rupit&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4-mile loop combining natural beauty with historical sites.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see on the trails?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty, characterized by a rocky mountain ridge, dense woodlands of beech and oak, and dramatic sheer cliffs. You'll encounter streams, the impressive Salt de Sallent waterfall, and panoramic views across the Montdois Plain and Ter Valley. The area is part of the Collsacabra Protected Natural Area, ensuring a diverse and well-preserved environment.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest along the hiking routes?
A: Absolutely. Beyond the natural wonders, you can explore historical sites. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e938472893/salto-de-sallent-and-sant-joan-de-fabregues-circular-from-rupit" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto de Sallent and Sant Joan de Fàbregues - Circular from Rupit&lt;/a&gt; trail passes by the 11th-century Romanesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4382081" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Feliuet de Savassona Hermitage&lt;/a&gt;. The charming medieval village of Rupit itself, with its cobbled streets and the iconic Suspension Bridge, serves as a captivating starting point for many excursions. You can also find the ruins of the 13th-century &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40326864" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Rupit&lt;/a&gt; overlooking the village.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Rupit I Pruit?
A: The trails in Rupit I Pruit are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 440 reviews. Hikers often praise the dramatic waterfalls, ancient woodlands, and rocky mountain ridges, as well as the variety of routes suitable for different ability levels.

Q: Is Rupit I Pruit suitable for family-friendly hikes?
A: Yes, Rupit I Pruit offers several easy trails that are suitable for families. The village itself is a charming place to explore, and many routes are relatively short and well-maintained, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1476688" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sallent Waterfall – Rupit Village loop from Rupit&lt;/a&gt;, which is 2.8 miles long and takes about 1 hour 25 minutes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Rupit I Pruit?
A: The region experiences moderate temperatures and abundant rainfall, fostering lush vegetation. While beautiful year-round, spring and autumn offer pleasant weather for hiking, with vibrant foliage in autumn and blooming flora in spring. After a rainy period, the Salt de Sallent waterfall is particularly impressive.

Q: Are there any trails that include the Rupit Suspension Bridge?
A: Yes, the iconic Rupit Suspension Bridge, known for its gentle sway, is a feature on some routes. You can experience it on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14958294" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Suspension Bridge of Rupit – Rupit Village loop from Hostal de la Devesa&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 6.8-mile trail.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking in Rupit I Pruit?
A: Rupit I Pruit is a popular destination, and parking is available within or near the village, which serves as a common starting point for many trails. It's advisable to arrive early, especially during peak seasons, to secure a spot.
tips: The Rupit stream is a tributary of the river Ter on its left. It is born from a set of springs and torrents that originate in the high areas of the Collsacabra mountain range. The stream begins to take shape after crossing the Tres Ulls bridge, at the Soler mill, where it forms a pool. After crossing the village of Rupit, the Rodó waterfall and the Pomareda, its waters are estimated by the Sallent waterfall, the most important element of its route. Under the jump the Devil's Gorge is formed and then the river crosses a wooded section of the Guilleries to flow into the Susqueda reservoir.

Rupit (officially part of Rupit i Pruit municipality) is a village in the county of Osona, in the subregion of the Collsacabra, in Catalonia, Spain. It is located at 822 m above sea level, 98 km from Barcelona.

Within the municipality of Rupit is this incredible waterfall with a vertical drop of more than 100 meters, the highest in Catalonia. It is advisable to visit it in the rainy season, as the drought affects its flow and sometimes there is no water.

Una cascada espectacular, sobre una roca igualmente impresionante.

A spectacular waterfall, on an equally impressive rock.
Lookout located at 936m altitude, very close to the anthropomorphic tombs. It has panoramic views of Agullola (921m), Sant Joan de Fàbregues, Salt de Sallent or the cliffs on both sides.

waypoints.name: View of the Salt de Sallent waterfall
